query: fluorescent overhead lighting institutional office interior unflattering harsh
message: anti_aesthetics/lighting: flat lighting — fluorescent institutional interiors, depressing offices/corridors, depthless cold light

response.objective_caption: A symmetrical interior view of a long institutional hallway lined with structural columns. On the left, tall narrow fluted columns are clad in small square tiles, while the right side features larger smooth cylindrical columns. Polished flooring reflects ceiling fixtures and pendant lights hanging from a paneled ceiling. At the far end, a set of glass entrance doors sits beneath a wide transom window displaying a circular crest or insignia. Dark doorways and posted notices interrupt the side walls along the corridor.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A symmetrical interior view of a long institutional hallway bathed in an intense sickly green color cast that tints every surface with an unnatural hue. Tall narrow fluted tile columns line the left wall and smooth cylindrical columns stand along the right, all overwhelmed by the monochrome green tone. Polished flooring reflects overhead pendant lights through the oppressive tint. At the far end, glass doors and a large transom window displaying a circular crest are submerged in the same unsettling green atmosphere, giving the empty corridor a feeling of contamination or unease.
